creator: jbeich@FreeBSD.org
text:
Modifying .am files without proper version[1] of automake or defining
USE_AUTOTOOLS may not always work. And so .am changes are not propagated
to .in files leading to confusion.

The origin of that reinplace line is a bit unclear to me. It was added
during gegl-0.1.2 supposedly[2] to help --disable-docs. But from my testing
--disable-docs comments out `docs' from SUBDIRS just fine in 0.1.2.

So, instead of skipping tests do something useful with them, like
actually running them before install.

[1] gegl-0.1.8 wants automake-1.11 while devel/automake provides automake-1.12
[2] nowadays, it tries to disable examples, tests, tools and can be written as

	${REINPLACE_CMD} -Ee '/^SUBDIRS/,/^$$/s/tools|examples|tests//g' \
		${WRKSRC}/Makefile.am ${WRKSRC}/Makefile.in

How-To-Repeat: $ make
===>  Building for gegl-0.1.8_3
 cd . && /bin/sh /usr/ports/graphics/gegl/work/gegl-0.1.8/missing --run automake-1.11 --gnu Makefile
/usr/ports/graphics/gegl/work/gegl-0.1.8/missing: automake-1.11: not found
WARNING: `automake-1.11' is missing on your system.  You should only need it if
         you modified `Makefile.am', `acinclude.m4' or `configure.ac'.
         You might want to install the `Automake' and `Perl' packages.
         Grab them from any GNU archive site.
 cd . && /bin/sh ./config.status Makefile
config.status: creating Makefile
[...]

$ ls $(make -V WRKSRC)/tools/.libs
img_cmp             introspect          operation_reference
